title: 10 best herbal capsules for vaginitis
intro_desc: Herbal capsules for vaginitis are natural supplements designed to alleviate symptoms of vaginitis, a condition characterized by inflammation and irritation of the vaginal area. These capsules help to soothe and heal the affected tissues, promoting a healthy balance of vaginal flora. The best herbal capsules for vaginitis include Calendula officinalis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Althaea officinalis, Aloe barbadensis, Cassia auriculata, Curcuma longa, Cassia alata, Urtica dioica, Aloe vera, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, vaginitis can lead to discomfort, pain, and anxiety, affecting daily activities, relationships, and overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with vaginitis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The herb, also known as Calendula officinalis, has been traditionally used to soothe and heal vaginal irritations, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with vaginitis. By taking herbal pot marigold capsules regularly, women can effectively treat and manage symptoms such as itching, burning, and discharge, promoting a healthy vaginal environment and overall well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold capsules contain calendula, which has anti-inflammatory compounds that can reduce swelling and discomfort associated with vaginal inflammation and irritation caused by vaginitis.

Antimicrobial: The capsules contain flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit antimicrobial properties, helping to combat infections caused by bacteria, yeast, or parasites that can contribute to vaginitis.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold's antioxidant activity may help protect the vaginal tissue from oxidative stress and damage, which can occur as a result of infection, inflammation, or other factors, exacerbating symptoms of vaginitis.
remedy_constituents: Triterpenoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate vaginitis symptoms by reducing inflammation and preventing the growth of pathogens that cause infection.
Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antioxidant and antimicrobial activities, which can help protect the vaginal mucosa from oxidative stress and infection, thereby reducing vaginitis symptoms.

Carotenoids (specifically, beta-carotene): These compounds have antioxidant properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the vaginal mucosa, thereby alleviating vaginitis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help alleviate symptoms of vaginitis.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which contribute to their therapeutic effects in treating vaginitis.
Roots: They are used due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in soothing and healing the affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried calendula officinalis flowers from a trusted source and store them in a cool dry place.
Weigh out 20g of dried flowers and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 50g of gelatin in a heat-proof bowl to form a uniform gel-like mixture.
Add 10ml of distilled water to the mixture and heat it in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the cooled mixture using a syringe or spoon and seal them immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe capsules helps with vaginitis because they contain bioactive compounds that soothe and calm irritated vaginal tissues. The anti-inflammatory properties of aloe vera help reduce inflammation, redness, and swelling associated with vaginitis. Additionally, the antifungal and antibacterial agents present in aloe capsules prevent the growth of harmful microorganisms that can cause infection. This natural remedy also promotes a healthy pH balance in the vagina, restoring its natural environment and reducing symptoms such as itching, burning, and discharge.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compound, aloin, helps reduce swelling and discomfort associated with vaginitis, providing relief from itching, burning, and pain.

Antimicrobial property: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of aloe vera help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause vaginitis, promoting healthy vaginal flora and reducing symptoms.

Healing and soothing property: Aloe vera's natural gel contains vitamins, minerals, and amino acids that promote wound healing and soothe irritated tissues in the vagina, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex sugars may help soothe and calm the vaginal lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with vaginitis.

Phenolic compounds (such as gallic acid and caffeic acid): These antioxidants may have antimicrobial properties, helping to prevent bacterial vaginosis and other infections that can lead to vaginitis.
Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone compound may have anti-inflammatory properties, which could help reduce vaginal inflammation and promote healing of the vaginal tissues.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The gel extracted from the leaves is primarily used due to its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: The gel also contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antimicrobial properties that help combat vaginitis-causing bacteria.
Leaves: The presence of vitamins A, C, and E in the leaf gel aids in healing and protecting the vaginal mucosa, making it a valuable ingredient for vaginitis treatment.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves to obtain gel for the herbal capsules.
Extract the aloe vera gel by slicing the leaves thinly and soaking them in water.
Mix the aloe vera gel with a natural gelatin to create a thick paste.
Fill a capsule machine with the aloe vera gel mixture and seal the capsules.
Store the aloe vera capsules in an airtight container at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle capsules helps with vaginitis because they contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that alleviate symptoms of vaginal infections. The capsules' ability to reduce inflammation in the vagina helps to eliminate discomfort, itching, and burning sensations associated with vaginitis. Additionally, stinging nettle's natural antimicrobial properties help to combat bacterial or fungal overgrowth, promoting a healthy balance of vaginal flora and reducing the risk of recurring infections. This natural remedy provides effective relief from symptoms without relying on harsh chemicals or antibiotics.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as polysaccharides and flavonoids, can reduce inflammation in the vagina, which helps to alleviate symptoms of vaginitis.

Antimicrobial properties: Nettle's antimicrobial properties, including its ability to inhibit bacterial growth and prevent fungal overgrowth, can help to combat underlying infections that contribute to vaginitis.
Hormone-balancing properties: Stinging nettle contains compounds that may help to regulate hormonal imbalances, which can contribute to vaginal dryness, itching, and other symptoms of vaginitis.
remedy_constituents: Amino Acids: Amino acids in Urtica dioica, such as histidine and tyrosine, may help with vaginitis by promoting the healing of damaged tissues and reducing inflammation in the vaginal area.
Phenolic Acids: Phenolic acids, including caffeic acid and ferulic acid, have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial vaginosis, a common cause of vaginitis.
Alkaloids: Alkaloids in Urtica dioica, such as stachydrine and valine, may help with vaginitis by reducing inflammation and modulating the immune response, thus alleviating symptoms of vaginitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them a potential remedy for soothing and treating vaginitis symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Urtica dioica contain compounds that may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of vaginitis.
Stems: The stems of the plant are also used due to their rich content of flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help to soothe and treat vaginitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh urtica dioica leaves in the early morning to prevent moisture loss and contamination.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 20 grams of dried urtica dioica leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 200 milligrams of the urtica dioica powder each.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower capsules helps with vaginitis because they contain Echinacea, a natural anti-inflammatory that soothes and calms irritated vaginal tissues. The herb's antifungal and antibacterial properties also help to combat infections caused by bacteria like Gardnerella vaginalis and Candida albicans, common culprits behind vaginitis symptoms such as itching, burning, and abnormal discharge. Additionally, purple coneflower's immune-boosting effects support the body's natural defenses against infection, promoting a healthy vaginal environment and reducing recurrence rates of vaginitis.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial and Antifungal Properties: Purple Coneflower's active compounds, such as triterpenoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that can cause vaginitis, reducing the risk of infection.

Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The plant's flavonoids and terpenes have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with vaginal infections, making it a natural treatment for vaginitis symptoms like itching, burning, and discharge.

Antioxidant Properties: Purple Coneflower's antioxidant compounds can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and damage caused by vaginal infections, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline Alkaloids: These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat bacterial vaginosis, a common cause of vaginitis.

Flavonoids (specifically Quercetin): Quercetin's anti-inflammatory properties can reduce inflammation and swelling in the vaginal area, alleviating symptoms of vaginitis such as itching and discharge.

Cichoric acid (a phenolic compound): Cichoric acid has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat infections and reduce inflammation in the vagina, making it an effective constituent in treating vaginitis.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high concentration of alkaloids, including echinacoside, which is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties beneficial for vaginitis.
Roots: The roots are used because they contain a higher concentration of polysaccharides, which are thought to have immunomodulatory effects that may help alleviate vaginitis symptoms.
Leaves: The leaves are used due to their content of caffeic acid, which has been shown to have antimicrobial properties that can help combat bacterial vaginosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in late summer or early fall for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested echinacea flowers and roots in a low-temperature oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of the dried echinacea flowers and roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 1 gram of the echinacea powder with 1 gram of cellulose powder as a capsule filler.
Fill 100 empty vegetable capsules with the echinacea and cellulose mixture using a capsule filler machine.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with vaginitis because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which help to soothe and calm the irritated vaginal tissue. The capsules contain compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of vaginitis, including itching, burning, and abnormal discharge. Additionally, licorice root has antimicrobial properties that can help to combat bacterial or fungal infections that may be contributing to the condition.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms such as itching, burning, and discharge associated with vaginitis.
Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in licorice root have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections caused by bacteria, yeast, or other microorganisms that contribute to vaginitis.
Saponins: Saponins in licorice root have antifungal and antibacterial effects, which can help eliminate the underlying cause of vaginitis, such as Candida overgrowth or bacterial imbalances.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to reduce the growth of pathogenic microorganisms and alleviate inflammation associated with vaginitis.

Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin, exhibits anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in reducing the severity of vaginitis symptoms such as itching, burning, and inflammation.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Glycyrrhiza glabra possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in protecting the vaginal mucosa from oxidative stress and inflammation caused by vaginitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound with anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate vaginitis symptoms.
Barks: The barks are used because they contain flavonoids and saponins, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help soothe and protect the vaginal mucosa.
Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain glycosides, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help reduce vaginitis symptoms and prevent infections.
remedy_recipe: Grind 500 grams of dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Sift the powder through a 100-mesh sieve to remove any large particles for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the sifted powder using a powder scoop for 10 minutes.
Seal the capsules using a capsule sealer to prevent the powder from spilling out for 2 minutes.
Label the capsules with the ingredients and dosages using a permanent marker for 5 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Prickly lettuce capsules helps with vaginitis because they contain a potent blend of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and bioflavonoids that work synergistically to soothe and calm the vaginal tissue. The herb's natural astringent properties help reduce inflammation and combat bacterial overgrowth, thereby alleviating symptoms such as itching, burning, and discharge associated with vaginitis. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Prickly lettuce capsules contain flavonoids and terpenes that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation associated with vaginitis.

Antioxidant properties: Prickly lettuce capsules contain antioxidants that help protect the vaginal tissue from oxidative damage and stress, which can contribute to vaginal health issues like vaginitis.
remedy_constituents: Lactucarium: Lactucarium, a sesquiterpene lactone, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of vaginitis, such as inflammation and infection.
Lactucopicrin: Lactucopicrin, another sesquiterpene lactone, has shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that contribute to vaginitis.

Furfuran lactones: Furan lactones, including those present in Lactuca virosa, have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with vaginitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of lactuca virosa flowers and leaves on a dry sunny day after 4 weeks of flowering.
Dry the harvested lactuca virosa plant material in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 20g of dried lactuca virosa plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground plant powder with 100g of gelatin capsules and 100g of silicon dioxide as a filler in a bowl.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a capsule filling machine.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for vaginitis due to their alleged anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots of Lactuca virosa are also used, possibly because of their potential to exhibit similar properties to the leaves.
Seeds: The seeds may be used due to their supposed antifungal and antibacterial properties, which could help alleviate vaginitis symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with vaginitis because they possess anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm the vaginal tissues, reducing discomfort and inflammation. The plant's diuretic properties also help to eliminate excess fluids and reduce bacterial growth, addressing one of the primary causes of vaginitis. Additionally, dandelion is rich in antioxidants, which combat free radicals and promote a healthy vaginal environment, preventing infections and promoting overall vaginal health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as taraxasterol and taraxerol, help reduce inflammation in the vaginal area, alleviating symptoms of vaginitis like swelling, redness, and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in dandelion, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, combat bacterial, fungal, and viral infections that can cause vaginitis, promoting a healthy vaginal microbiome.

remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Taraxacum officinale, such as quercetin, possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate the inflammation and infection associated with vaginitis.

Polysaccharides: The polysaccharide components in Taraxacum officinale, including inulin and arabinogalactan, have prebiotic properties that can promote the growth of beneficial gut bacteria, contributing to a healthier vaginal microbiome and potentially reducing the risk of vaginitis.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol, a triterpenoid saponin found in Taraxacum officinale, has been reported to have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause vaginitis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm vaginal tissues.
Roots: Contain inulin and other compounds that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in the vaginal area.

remedy_recipe: Harvest taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in late summer or early fall when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a single layer at 150 fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried taraxacum officinale roots and leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed blender or coffee grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of taraxacum officinale powder with 100 milligrams of gelatin powder and 10 milligrams of vitamin e oil.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the taraxacum officinale mixture and seal them according to the manufacturer's instructions.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek capsules helps with vaginitis because of their antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The herb contains compounds like galactomannan, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of Candida albicans, a common cause of vaginal infections. Additionally, fenugreek's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the vagina, promoting healing and recovery from vaginitis symptoms such as itching, burning, and abnormal discharge. The capsules also have a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, providing relief from discomfort and promoting a healthy vaginal environment.

Antibacterial and antifungal properties: The capsule's herbal extract exhibits antibacterial and antifungal properties, which can help combat bacterial and fungal infections that cause vaginitis, such as Candida albicans.

Soother of the genitourinary tract: Fenugreek has been traditionally used to soothe and calm the genitourinary tract, helping to reduce symptoms of vaginitis like discharge, odor, and pain during urination or sexual intercourse.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, helping to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial vaginosis, a common cause of vaginitis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest trigonella foenum graecum flowers and leaves, ensuring a minimum of 500 grams of dried plant material is collected.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator at 40°c for 24 hours to prevent damage.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or spice grinder for 30 minutes.
Mix the powder with a binding agent such as gum arabic and fill empty gelatin capsules with 250 milligrams of the mixture per capsule.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dry place for up to 12 months.

The mucilages present in marshmallow extract form a gel-like barrier that shields the delicate vaginal lining from further irritation, allowing it to heal naturally. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow help to calm the symptoms of vaginitis, such as itching, burning, and painful urination, providing relief and promoting overall vaginal health.

remedy_parts: Roots: Althaea officinalis roots are used to make capsules for vaginitis due to their high mucilage content, which provides soothing and protective properties to irritated tissues.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried althaea officinalis root material from a clean environment at the correct time of year.
Grind the harvested material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about 5 minutes.
Mix 200-400 mg of the ground material with 100-200 mg of a filler material in a bowl.
Fill 50-100 vegetable capsules with the mixture and seal them using a capsule filling machine.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Highbush cranberry capsules helps with vaginitis because of its potent antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The capsule's active ingredients, such as epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG), have been shown to combat bacterial vaginosis by disrupting the biofilm that allows bacteria to thrive in the vaginal canal. Additionally, highbush cranberry's antifungal properties can help alleviate symptoms of yeast infections, while its anti-inflammatory effects may reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with vaginitis. remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Highbush cranberry capsules contain antimicrobial compounds that help to eliminate Candida and other microorganisms that can cause vaginitis, promoting a healthy balance of vaginal flora.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The capsules' anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with vaginitis, providing relief from symptoms such as itching, burning, and swelling.

remedy_constituents: Proanthocyanidins (PACs): These compounds help prevent bacterial adhesion to the vaginal epithelium, reducing the risk of vaginitis caused by bacteria such as Escherichia coli.
Quinic acid derivatives: These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce bacterial growth and inflammation associated with vaginitis.
Epicatechin: This flavonoid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of vaginitis, such as itching, burning, and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used because of their proanthocyanidins, which help prevent bacterial adhesion and prevent vaginitis.
Leaves: They are used because of their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and prevent vaginitis.
Barks: They are used because of their antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which help prevent bacterial vaginitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of vaccinium macrocarpon berries at their ripest stage when the color is deep purple.
Clean the harvested berries thoroughly with distilled water to remove dirt and impurities immediately.
Dry the cleaned vaccinium macrocarpon berries in a temperature-controlled environment at 40 degrees celsius for 24 hours.
Grind the dried vaccinium macrocarpon berries into a fine powder using a coffee grinder with a 1:10 ratio.
Fill 100 empty gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the vaccinium macrocarpon powder each at a speed of 500 capsules per hour.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Field horsetail capsules helps with vaginitis because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The silicates present in horsetail capsules help to soothe the vaginal tissue, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with vaginitis. remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry equisetum arvense leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated for maximum potency.
Grind 30 grams of dried equisetum arvense leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the equisetum arvense powder with 60 grams of tapioca starch and 30 grams of gelatin in a bowl for proper encapsulation.
Fill empty #00 capsules with the herbal mixture and seal them immediately to prevent contamination and oxidation.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature away from direct sunlight and moisture for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horehound capsules helps with vaginitis because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated vaginal tissues. The herb's antimicrobial compounds also help combat bacterial or fungal infections that can cause vaginitis. Additionally, horehound's demulcent properties create a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, shielding them from further irritation and promoting healing. Anti-inflammatory properties: The plant contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of allergies, sinusitis, and other inflammatory conditions.
Antimicrobial properties: Horehound has been found to exhibit antibacterial and antifungal activity against various microorganisms, which may help combat infections and promote wound healing.

remedy_recipe: Harvest marrubium vulgare leaves from a trusted source or purchase dried leaves from a reputable supplier.
Dry the fresh leaves using a dehydrator or air drying method for at least 24 hours.
Grind 250mg of dried marrubium vulgare leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered herb with 1000mg of silica gel capsules or empty hard-shell capsules and fill them using a capsule filler machine.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months or refrigerate for longer storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild potato capsules helps with vaginitis because they contain compounds that have potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The extracts from the wild potato plant, such as Dioscorea villosa, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the vagina and combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause vaginitis. Additionally, these capsules may help to restore the natural balance of vaginal flora, which is often disrupted in cases of vaginitis, leading to a reduction in symptoms and improved overall health.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: The capsules' antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria, fungi, and yeast that cause vaginitis, reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Wild Potato capsules have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling, pain, and discomfort associated with vaginitis, making it an effective natural remedy for managing symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in the capsules help protect the vagina from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of vaginitis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated vaginal tissue.
Rhyzomes: Contain antifungal properties that combat Candida overgrowth and promote healthy vaginal flora.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry fresh taraxacum kok-saghyz root for a minimum of 48 hours using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind the dried taraxacum kok-saghyz root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powdered taraxacum kok-saghyz root with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler to create an infusion.
Simmer the infusion for 20 minutes, then let it cool and strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Fill gelatin capsules with one gram of the cooled and strained taraxacum kok-saghyz root infusion and store them in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Golden sagewort capsules helps with vaginitis because it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that aid in soothing irritated tissues and combating bacterial overgrowth. The herb's flavonoids and phenolic acids work synergistically to reduce inflammation, ease discomfort, and promote healing of the vaginal mucosa. Additionally, golden sagewort has been traditionally used to treat yeast infections and other vaginal infections, making it a natural and effective adjunctive therapy for vaginitis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Golden Sagewort has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity against various types of bacteria and fungi, helping to combat infections that can contribute to vaginitis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the vagina, which is a common symptom of vaginitis, and alleviate discomfort and pain associated with the condition.

Antioxidant properties: Golden Sagewort's antioxidant activity may help protect vaginal tissues from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy tissue maintenance and reducing the risk of chronic infections.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Golden Sagewort plant contain bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and combat bacterial infections associated with vaginitis.
Seeds: Golden Sagewort seeds are rich in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making them effective in soothing irritated vaginal tissues and preventing infection recurrence.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried stillingia sylvatica root bark and grind it into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Mix the stillingia sylvatica root bark powder with 250 milligrams of gelatin in a bowl until well combined for 5 minutes.
Add 100 milliliters of distilled water to the mixture and stir well, allowing it to sit at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Pour the mixture into empty vegetable capsules and seal them tightly, allowing them to dry for 24 hours before storage.
